hypothesis: Integrating a structured semantic memory module that encodes domain-specific heuristics (e.g., canonical table parsing rules, typical cutoff and charge-assignment defaults, and unit/format normalization) into the multi-agent pipeline will reduce the rate of incorrectly assigned numerical force-field parameters by at least 50% and raise the average Intersection-over-Union (IoU) of extracted force-field parameters across a benchmark of 100 literature papers with diverse table formats from ≈0.90 to ≥0.97 in a single-pass extraction.
